_comment: Reconciled, single source of truth for the valueSource vocabulary siera resolves. 'simple' = direct ARS metadata lookup; 'complex' = an assembled argument/list string. The 'supported' flag MUST match .supported_value_sources() (operation_N is matched by pattern). Legacy docs also referenced trt_filter_stm, num_grp_any, anset_cond_stm, fisher_cond_stm and AG_ds1 - siera does NOT resolve these, so they are intentionally absent here.

constructs.details: Analysis ID of the referenced denominator analysis
First grouping variable of the referenced denominator analysis
TRUE/FALSE: whether the highest resultsByGroup grouping is data-driven
All active grouping variables plus the analysis variable, comma separated, unquoted (for dplyr::distinct)
All grouping variables as a quoted, comma-separated list (to be wrapped in c(...)). Stamps CDISC group metadata. Preferred for grouped tabulations.
All grouping variables as an UNQUOTED, comma-separated list. Same vars as by_listc but without quotes.

Leading-comma 'by/variables' argument string: the last grouping goes to variables=, the rest to by=. Stamps CDISC group metadata. The raw token is invalid R until substituted (it injects a leading comma).
Leading-comma 'strata/variables' argument string (data-driven analogue of by_vars). Stamps CDISC group metadata.

Leading-comma 'by = ...' argument string (all groupings to by=). CAUTION: resolves to n_group_cols = 0, so siera stamps NO group[n] metadata columns - do not use where CDISC-compliant group metadata is required; prefer by_listc / by_vars / strata_vars.
operation_1 ... operation_N: the method's own operation IDs in 'order'. Matched by pattern (^operation_[0-9]+$), never declared as a parameter.
